text.title: Budget Optimization Antipatterns
text.summary: Common budget allocation mistakes and their fixes.

antipattern.description: Equal budget distribution across all campaigns regardless of performance.
antipattern.reason: Ignores efficiency variance; funds losing campaigns at the same rate as winning ones, capping overall ROAS.

antipattern.description: Doubling a campaign budget in one step when performance looks good.
antipattern.reason: Triggers learning phase reset; CPA spikes 20-80% for 3-7 days, erasing the efficiency gains that prompted the increase.

antipattern.description: Eliminating the testing budget during periods of pressure to hit efficiency targets.
antipattern.reason: Destroys the innovation pipeline; proven campaigns eventually plateau, and without tests there are no future winners to scale.

antipattern.description: Cutting a campaign after 3-4 days of poor performance.
antipattern.reason: Insufficient data; platform algorithms need 7-14 days minimum and 50+ conversions before performance stabilizes — early data is high-variance noise.

antipattern.description: Optimizing CPA without tracking downstream quality (lead-to-close rate, LTV).
antipattern.reason: A $20 CPA from a channel that produces 2% close rates is worse than a $100 CPA from a channel with 20% close rates — cost-per-customer matters, not cost-per-lead.
